stories.title: D20 Board parts ways with superintendent Jinger Haberer

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text: D20 families: Academy District 20's Board has parted ways with Superintendent Jinger Haberer, effective May 15. Board said it lost confidence in her leadership. A superintendent search is coming. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text: What D20 families need to know: D20 says operations remain stable and schools continue normally. No reason was given for Superintendent Haberer's removal. A superintendent search is coming. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
D20 families, there's a significant leadership change you should know about.

Academy District 20's Board of Education has parted ways with Superintendent Jinger Haberer, effective May 15. Board President Amy Shandy said the board "unanimously determined that we do not have confidence" in her leadership moving forward.

The announcement came via email to parents and guardians. No specific reason for the decision was made public. According to the district, the departure follows a contractually allowable hearing process.

Haberer had been in the role since March 2023, bringing 35 years of education experience from districts in Washington, Aurora, and Fort Collins. As of this week, she has vacated the superintendent position.

The district is reassuring D20 families that schools are operating as usual and the leadership team — assistant superintendents, principals, and teachers — are continuing their work without disruption. A search for a new superintendent is expected to begin soon.

This affects thousands of families across north Colorado Springs and the Monument area. We'll keep you updated as more information becomes available.

What questions do you have about what this means for D20 families?
facebook.image_caption: Major news for D20 families: Academy District 20's Board has parted ways with Superintendent Haberer. The district says it remains stable and operations continue as normal. A superintendent search is coming.

article.headline: D20 Parts Ways With Superintendent Haberer: What COS Families Need to Know

stories.title: CMZoo Centennial + New Giraffe Center opening this summer

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text: Cheyenne Mountain Zoo turns 100 in 2026 — and this summer brings a brand-new Giraffe Center. 🦒 The $40M facility has a 12,000 sq. ft. barn with the largest ETFE clear roof over any animal exhibit in North America. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text: Summer field trip alert: CMZoo's new Giraffe Center opens this summer. 11 feeding zones (up from 3), 25% more outdoor space, and a weeklong opening celebration. The COS family outing of 2026. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
COS families — this is the summer field trip you're going to want to put on the calendar now.

Cheyenne Mountain Zoo turns 100 this year. Founded in 1926 by Spencer Penrose, CMZoo has been a Colorado Springs family institution for a full century. And to mark the occasion, this summer brings the grand opening of something spectacular: the International Center for the Care and Conservation of Giraffe.

Here's what this new Giraffe Center means for your next zoo visit:

— It's a $40 million facility funded through zoo revenue and donations
— The giraffe barn is 12,000 square feet with a clear ETFE roof — the largest of its kind covering any animal exhibit in North America
— Outdoor space for the giraffes increases by 25%
— Feeding zones expand from 3 to 11 — more chances for kiddos to get up close
— A new feeding tower encourages giraffes to roam a hilly outdoor area

The zoo will hold a weeklong celebration when the new center officially opens. CMZoo is also welcoming a new President and CEO this year, making 2026 a genuinely historic year for one of COS's most beloved family destinations.

We'll share the grand opening date as soon as it's announced. In the meantime, if you haven't been to CMZoo recently, this is the year to go.

What's your favorite memory from Cheyenne Mountain Zoo?
facebook.image_caption: 100 years of Cheyenne Mountain Zoo — and this summer brings a brand-new $40M Giraffe Center with 11 feeding zones and a one-of-a-kind clear roof. The ultimate COS family outing of 2026. 🦒

article.headline: Cheyenne Mountain Zoo Turns 100: New Giraffe Center Opens This Summer

stories.title: Safe Streets COS — school zone safety upgrades citywide

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text: Colorado Springs added 22 school zones this year through the Safe Streets COS initiative. 🚸 Mann Middle already has improvements. Sierra High flashers go live fall 2026. Safer drop-off ahead. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
Good news if you do school drop-off or pickup in Colorado Springs: the city is making school zones significantly safer.

The City of Colorado Springs officially launched the "Safe Streets COS" initiative on May 13, focusing on reducing traffic crashes around schools and throughout the city. The launch event highlighted improvements at Mann Middle School, and by the end of this school year, 22 additional school zones have been added citywide.

What that means practically:

— Better signage, safer crossings, and reduced speed zones near schools
— Sierra High School: new flashers installed, operational at the start of the 2026-27 school year
— Panorama Middle School: improvements underway
— The program combines infrastructure upgrades, enforcement tools, and public education

The long-term goal is to reduce serious injury and deadly crashes by 35% by 2035. That's a meaningful target — and these school-zone improvements are part of getting there.

If you drop off or pick up at one of these schools, you may already be seeing some of the changes. More are coming across the city before the next school year starts.

Which school do you drive to most often? Have you noticed any improvements yet?
facebook.image_caption: Colorado Springs added 22 school zones this year through Safe Streets COS. Safer drop-off, safer pickup, safer streets for COS kids this fall.

article.headline: Safe Streets COS: 22 New School Zones and What It Means for Drop-Off This Fall

stories.title: AAP's new screen time guidance — quality over hours

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text: The AAP updated its screen time guidance in 2026. New focus: quality and context, not just hours. Under 18 months: no screens. Ages 2-5: 1 hour of quality content. Ages 6+: quality over the clock. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
The American Academy of Pediatrics updated its screen time guidance in 2026 — and the biggest shift is that it's no longer just about the clock.

The new framework from the AAP focuses on three questions rather than strict hour limits:

1. Is the content high quality and age-appropriate?
2. Does screen time displace healthy activities (sleep, movement, face-to-face time)?
3. Are parents watching or engaged with kids during screen time?

The specifics still matter for younger kids: no screens for children under 18 months (video calls are the exception). Ages 2-5: up to 1 hour of high-quality content daily. But for kids 6 and up, the guidance shifts to quality and context over raw minutes.

The AAP is also warning about "digital ecosystems" — apps, platforms, and devices specifically designed to maximize engagement — and their impact on children's attention, sleep, and development.

One practical takeaway: the AAP now recommends that families create a Family Media Plan — shared household rules about where screens are used, when, and what for. There are templates at HealthyChildren.org.

This isn't about guilt. It's about being thoughtful with one of the most powerful tools in our kids' lives.

How does your family handle screen time? We're always curious what actually works in real COS households.
facebook.image_caption: The AAP updated screen time guidance in 2026: it's now about quality, context, and what screens displace — not just the clock. Here's what COS families need to know.

article.headline: AAP's Updated Screen Time Guidance: It's No Longer Just About the Clock

research.facts:
AAP Screen Time / Digital Ecosystems Policy
**Sources:**
- CHOC (Children's Health): "Updated AAP recommendations for screen time: What parents need to know"
- HeyOtto: "Screen Time for Kids 2026: The Complete Parent's Guide"
- AAP/Pediatrics: "Digital Ecosystems, Children, and Adolescents: Policy Statement" (Pediatrics, Volume 157)
- Levin Law: "Pediatricians warn digital ecosystems are harming children and teens"
**Verified facts (HIGH confidence):**
- AAP Digital Ecosystems policy: January 2026 (from Pediatrics Vol. 157, Issue 2) — confirmed
- No screens under 18 months except video calls — confirmed
- Ages 2-5: limit to 1 hour high-quality content daily — confirmed
- Ages 6+: quality, context, and displacement of healthy activities are key — confirmed
- Family media plans recommended — confirmed AAP + CHOC
- Policy warns about commercial digital ecosystem design that prioritizes engagement over children's health — confirmed
- Displacement concern: screens displacing sleep, movement, in-person relationships — confirmed
**MEDIUM confidence:**
- Specific shift "away from strict hour limits toward quality/context" was the explicit framing — confirmed CHOC article

stories.title: D49 Student Success Center opening August — new preschool spots

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text: D49 families: District 49's new Student Success Center opens in August 2026 with two full-time preschool classrooms. Mon-Thu schedule, Base49 care on Fridays. New spots for families who couldn't access part-day programs. #COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

facebook.long_form:
D49 families planning for fall: there's a new early childhood education option opening in Colorado Springs.

District 49 is opening a Student Success Center in August 2026, at the start of the 2026-27 school year. Inside, they're adding two brand-new full-time preschool classrooms — a significant expansion of D49's early childhood education program.

Here's what the program offers:

— Full-time preschool, Monday through Thursday
— Before- and after-school care through a partnership with Base49
— Friday childcare through Base49 as well

The director of D49's Early Childhood Education program, Stacey Powell, explained the goal directly: "The addition of two full-time preschool classrooms, along with our valued Base49 partnership, allows our program to expand access to high-quality early childhood education for families who have not been able to take advantage of our part-day programming."

The Student Success Center will also house transition services for students ages 18-21, therapy teams, and special education support — making it a comprehensive hub for students who need additional support.

If you're a D49 family looking for full-time preschool for fall 2026, this is worth checking out. Visit d49.org for enrollment information.

Are you a D49 family? We'd love to know what you're most looking forward to in the new school year.
facebook.image_caption: D49 families: two new full-time preschool classrooms are opening this August at the new Student Success Center. Mon–Thu schedule with Base49 care on Fridays. More early childhood spots for COS families who need them.

article.headline: D49 Opening New Preschool Seats This Fall at the Student Success Center
